---
title: "Water Cowbane (Tiedemannia filiformis)"
slug: "tiedemannia-filiformis"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474834911/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Water Cowbane"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Apiales"
tax_family: "Apiaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Apiales", "Apiaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474834911/medium.jpg" alt="Water Cowbane" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/willforestbeck">
willforestbeck</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264405061">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Apiales
-   **Family:** Apiaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=482408)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Common across Florida.

## Natural history notes

A wetland wildflower characterized by its finely divided, thread-like leaves and small white flower clusters typical of the carrot family. In Florida, it is found in shallow water and along the edges of ponds, marshes, and ditches. It grows during the warmer months and provides structure in aquatic habitats, though like many members of its family, it can be toxic if ingested.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/482408-Tiedemannia-filiformis)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-tiedemannia-filiformis/)
